methodology: Treaty ratification status from UN Treaty Collection. 'Major non-party' here means a state of substantial geopolitical weight that has not ratified.
data_status: illustrative — reflects public ratification status as of last verification

treaties.name: Rome Statute (International Criminal Court)
treaties.scope: Jurisdiction over genocide, war crimes, crimes against humanity, aggression
treaties.notes: USA signed (2000) and unsigned (2002). Russia withdrew its signature (2016). Several African states have threatened or initiated withdrawal.

treaties.name: Convention Against Torture
treaties.scope: Absolute prohibition; mandatory state reporting; Optional Protocol allows inspections
treaties.notes: Most major powers are parties to CAT; the Optional Protocol allowing inspection has many fewer parties.

treaties.name: Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities
treaties.notes: US signed (2009), Senate failed to ratify (2012).

treaties.name: International Covenant on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights
major_non_parties: ZAF (signed but not ratified Op Protocol)

treaties.name: Convention on the Rights of the Child
treaties.notes: Only UN member state that has not ratified.

treaties.name: Convention on Cluster Munitions
treaties.notes: Major arms producers/users have largely declined to join.

treaties.name: Anti-Personnel Mine Ban Convention (Ottawa)
treaties.notes: USA is the largest non-party despite stockpile reductions.

treaties.name: Treaty on the Prohibition of Nuclear Weapons
International Convention for the Protection of All Persons from Enforced Disappearance
treaties.notes: Disappearance is a category most accountability regimes underweight; few major powers are parties.
